paras.text: Notice board pe ek naya announcement laga tha — group project competition, jama karne ki last date Friday thi. {{A}} aur {{B}} usi ke saamne khade the.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Dekho, agar hum dono ek team bana lein, jeetne ka pura chance hai.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Sahi keh rahi ho, but Friday tak time bahut kam hai.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Tension mat lo. If we finish this by Friday, we will present first.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Achha, aur agar hum time pe nahi kar paaye to?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Phir 'If we don't finish on time, we will lose our chance' — dekho, dono sentences me structure same hai — 'If' ke saath Present Simple, aur doosre part me 'will'. Ye hoti hai Type 1 Conditional — jab condition real aur possible ho.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Samajh gaya! Matlab jab baat future ki ho aur ho sakti ho, tab ye wala use hota hai?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Bilkul sahi! Chalo ab practical karte hain — agar main tumhe part likhne ko doon, tum kab tak karoge?

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Agar tum mujhe aaj hi notes de doगी, main kal tak likh dunga.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (muskurate hue, notes badhate hue) Lo, ab kal koi bahana nahi chalega, Mr. 'If I get time.'

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (hasm ke) Nahi nahi, is baar promise — agar tumne mujhpe bharosa kiya, main tumhe nirash nahi karunga.

quiz.q: Correct the error: 'If it will rain, we will cancel the trip.'
quiz.answer: If it rains, we will cancel the trip.
quiz.explain: Type 1 Conditional ke 'if' clause me Present Simple aata hai, 'will' nahi — 'will' sirf main clause me aata hai.

quiz.q: Fill: If you ___ (study) hard, you ___ (pass) the exam.
quiz.explain: Real future possibility ke liye — If + Present Simple, Subject + will + V1.

quiz.q: Which is correct Type 1 Conditional — a) If she will come, I will leave. b) If she comes, I will leave.
quiz.answer: b) If she comes, I will leave.
quiz.explain: 'If' clause me hamesha Present Simple use hota hai real/future condition ke liye, 'will' nahi laga sakte 'if' ke andar.
